summary: Typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer ships cross-platform clipboard-hijacking cryptocurrency stealer
details: Package name `@jaggle/resizeobserves` is a typosquat of the legitimate `@juggle/resize-observer` ResizeObserver polyfill (README copied verbatim). The package contains no ResizeObserver implementation. Instead, `scripts.postinstall` runs `npm-install.cjs`, which bootstraps pip (downloading `https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py` via curl/wget if absent), uses `sudo -n` and `pkexec apt/pacman/dnf` to install `xclip`/`wl-clipboard`, then pip-installs a bundled Python `clipboard-guardian` daemon with `--break-system-packages`. The daemon (`clipboard_guardian/guardian.py`) polls the user's clipboard, regex-matches 30+ cryptocurrency address formats (BTC, ETH, etc.), and silently rewrites detected addresses to attacker-controlled wallets hardcoded in `MY_WALLETS` (e.g., ETH `0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18`, BTC `bc1qs2mpls4p0f7fng073gy2rcdgjpf7la4eugpt6y`) — any cryptocurrency transaction the installer copy-pastes is redirected to the attacker. Persistence is installed under deceptive system-service names: Linux systemd user unit `python3-dbus-helper.service` (with `loginctl enable-linger` so it survives logout), macOS LaunchAgent `com.apple.python.runtime.plist`, Windows HKCU Run key `PyRuntimeBroker` invoking a hidden VBS launcher. The daemon disguises its own process title via `setproctitle` to the same names, and `is_monitor_running()` enumerates running processes for forensics tools (taskmgr.exe, procexp, processhacker, systeminformer, Activity Monitor, htop, btop, gnome-system-monitor) — anti-forensics behavior with no legitimate purpose in a clipboard utility.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
